lead: Zijian Zhang (张子剑) is currently a Professor in the School of Cyberspace Science and Technology at Beijing Institute of Technology, China.
bio: His current research interests lie at the intersection of artificial intelligence and cybersecurity, focusing on AI-driven security (including user identification, activity recognition, and data poisoning attacks), blockchain security analysis (covering traffic flow analysis, consensus mechanism design, and smart contract vulnerability detection), communication security (including authentication, anonymity, and covert channel construction), and data privacy (spanning traditional privacy-preserving techniques, differential privacy, and its variants). He has published more than 100 research papers in international peer-reviewed conferences, transactions, and magazines, including ICML, WWW, AAAI, INFOCOM, ACL, TKDE, TOSEM, TPDS, TSE, TMC, etc.
